mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is useful for ensuring the security of your applications by scanning images and running security assessments on your Kubernetes resources. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and the potential performance impact during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm repository.
> This command updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Create a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Install the Trivy Operator in the trivy-system namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> List the pods in the trivy-system namespace to ensure they are running.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration resource
steps.description:
This step creates a TrivyConfiguration resource to define how the Trivy Operator should scan images.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: my-registry-secret
EOF

```

> This command creates a TrivyConfiguration resource.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: trivy-config
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: my-registry-secret
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all configurations and data related to the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstall the Trivy Operator from the trivy-system namespace.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfigurations -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfig-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backup existing TrivyConfiguration resources.
> Upgrade the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyconfigurations -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfig-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivy-operator not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Trivy Operator is installed in the correct namespace using 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The image specified in the TrivyConfiguration may not be accessible due to incorrect imagePullSecrets.

**Fix:**
Check the imagePullSecrets in your TrivyConfiguration and ensure they are correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivyconfiguration.trivy.aquasecurity.github.io is forbidden'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient RBAC permissions for the user or service account.

**Fix:**
Ensure the user/service account has the necessary permissions to create TrivyConfiguration resources.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be misconfigured or there may be an issue with the underlying Kubernetes resources.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Trivy Operator pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image scanning configurations and improved performance for large clusters.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from v0.22.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' to verify.

    - name: my-registry-secret
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
